title: melicope micrococca
intro: Melicope micrococca, also known as the yellow Hawea, is a small shrub native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as improved wound healing, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, Melicope micrococca is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
